mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income expectations and weakens consumer demand, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 30 days
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in durable goods consumption and residential investment, which amplifies downside earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample tests, or forward returns do not systematically decline when both series contract concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During a financial stress regime, a sharp drop in industrial production reflects supply chain disruptions and inventory destocking, which triggers immediate risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correlate with negative 5-day forward returns during stress periods, or the signal reverses direction when financial volatility exceeds historical norms.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential contraction in real GDP growth reduces aggregate corporate revenue expectations, forcing analysts to lower earnings forecasts and depressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral during confirmed GDP contractions, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX returns exceeds zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5 percent, conventional monetary policy loses traction and signals severe economic weakness, prompting a repricing of risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when the policy rate breaches the threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in nonfarm payrolls growth and industrial production indicates a broad-based economic slowdown that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not improve predictive accuracy over individual series, or forward returns fail to decline when both labor and supply indicators weaken simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market collapse regime, a sharp decline in housing starts accelerates wealth destruction and consumer deleveraging, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
regime_hint: housing market collapse and balance sheet recession
failure_condition: Housing starts declines do not precede negative 5-day forward returns during the specified regime, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when adjusted for inflation and interest rates.
